verses.text: To avoid fornication, 'let every man have his own wife.' Marriage's place. It is a safeguard God ordained.
To avoid fornication, 'let every man have his own wife.' Marriage's place. It is a safeguard God ordained.
Render due benevolence; defraud not one another, except by consent for a time, 'that ye may give yourselves to... prayer.' Mutual duty. Husband and wife owe each other faithful love.
Render due benevolence; defraud not one another, except by consent for a time, 'that ye may give yourselves to... prayer.' Mutual duty. Husband and wife owe each other faithful love.
Render due benevolence; defraud not one another, except by consent for a time, 'that ye may give yourselves to... prayer.' Mutual duty. Husband and wife owe each other faithful love.
I would all were as I am, 'but every man hath his proper gift of God.' Different gifts. Singleness and marriage are each God's gift.
I would all were as I am, 'but every man hath his proper gift of God.' Different gifts. Singleness and marriage are each God's gift.
To the unmarried, it is good to abide as I; 'but if they cannot contain, let them marry.' Wise counsel. Marriage is better than burning desire.
To the unmarried, it is good to abide as I; 'but if they cannot contain, let them marry.' Wise counsel. Marriage is better than burning desire.
Let not the wife depart, nor the husband put away his wife. Binding bond. The Lord forbids the breaking of marriage.
Let not the wife depart, nor the husband put away his wife. Binding bond. The Lord forbids the breaking of marriage.
If an unbelieving spouse is content to dwell, part not; 'the unbelieving... is sanctified by the' believer. Kept together. The believer's presence blesses the home.
If an unbelieving spouse is content to dwell, part not; 'the unbelieving... is sanctified by the' believer. Kept together. The believer's presence blesses the home.
If an unbelieving spouse is content to dwell, part not; 'the unbelieving... is sanctified by the' believer. Kept together. The believer's presence blesses the home.
If the unbeliever departs, let him; 'God hath called us to peace.' Released. Faith is not bound to one who forsakes it.
If the unbeliever departs, let him; 'God hath called us to peace.' Released. Faith is not bound to one who forsakes it.
Walk as God has called; 'circumcision is nothing... but the keeping of the commandments of God.' Inward thing. The heart's obedience, not the outward mark, matters.
Walk as God has called; 'circumcision is nothing... but the keeping of the commandments of God.' Inward thing. The heart's obedience, not the outward mark, matters.
Walk as God has called; 'circumcision is nothing... but the keeping of the commandments of God.' Inward thing. The heart's obedience, not the outward mark, matters.
Abide in the calling wherein ye were called; 'ye are bought with a price.' Content where God set us. Each may serve God in his own place.
Abide in the calling wherein ye were called; 'ye are bought with a price.' Content where God set us. Each may serve God in his own place.
Abide in the calling wherein ye were called; 'ye are bought with a price.' Content where God set us. Each may serve God in his own place.
Abide in the calling wherein ye were called; 'ye are bought with a price.' Content where God set us. Each may serve God in his own place.
Abide in the calling wherein ye were called; 'ye are bought with a price.' Content where God set us. Each may serve God in his own place.
For 'the present distress' it is good to remain; 'but and if thou marry, thou hast not sinned.' Liberty. Singleness is wise in hard times, but marriage is no sin.
For 'the present distress' it is good to remain; 'but and if thou marry, thou hast not sinned.' Liberty. Singleness is wise in hard times, but marriage is no sin.
For 'the present distress' it is good to remain; 'but and if thou marry, thou hast not sinned.' Liberty. Singleness is wise in hard times, but marriage is no sin.
For 'the present distress' it is good to remain; 'but and if thou marry, thou hast not sinned.' Liberty. Singleness is wise in hard times, but marriage is no sin.
'The time is short'; use this world 'as not abusing it'; 'the fashion of this world passeth away.' Hold loosely. Earthly things are passing and must not enslave us.
'The time is short'; use this world 'as not abusing it'; 'the fashion of this world passeth away.' Hold loosely. Earthly things are passing and must not enslave us.
'The time is short'; use this world 'as not abusing it'; 'the fashion of this world passeth away.' Hold loosely. Earthly things are passing and must not enslave us.
The unmarried cares for the Lord; the married for the world; I would you serve Him 'without distraction.' Undivided heart. Singleness can free one for fuller devotion.
The unmarried cares for the Lord; the married for the world; I would you serve Him 'without distraction.' Undivided heart. Singleness can free one for fuller devotion.
The unmarried cares for the Lord; the married for the world; I would you serve Him 'without distraction.' Undivided heart. Singleness can free one for fuller devotion.
The unmarried cares for the Lord; the married for the world; I would you serve Him 'without distraction.' Undivided heart. Singleness can free one for fuller devotion.
He that gives his virgin in marriage does well; he that gives her not 'doeth better.' Both lawful. Each choice may be good before God.
He that gives his virgin in marriage does well; he that gives her not 'doeth better.' Both lawful. Each choice may be good before God.
He that gives his virgin in marriage does well; he that gives her not 'doeth better.' Both lawful. Each choice may be good before God.
The wife is bound while her husband lives; if he die, she is free, 'only in the Lord.' Freedom in Christ. The widow may remarry, but to a believer.
